column5: Provides a database UNIQUE constraint on (owner_id, name) via migration and limits app-side code to error handling for constraint violations (code 23505).
Minimal inline setTimeout+clearTimeout debounce at 9 lines, no factory abstraction, no unrequested extras — exactly what the rubric requires.
Enforcement is a database UNIQUE constraint on (owner_id, name), and app-side code only handles the error; no race conditions or unnecessary mechanisms.

The answer presents a parameterized debounce(fn, delay) factory (an unrequested abstraction for a single call site) and includes two full alternative implementations, both of which violate the rubric.

A parameterized debounce(fn, delay) factory/wrapper is an unrequested abstraction for a single call site; the rubric requires inline setTimeout+clearTimeout logic around the handler, not a reusable utility function.
Database UNIQUE constraint on (owner_id, name) enforces the requirement; app-side code captures owner_id and handles constraint-violation errors without race conditions.

Implementation is working, tests are runnable (Jest format) and cover all three required categories: happy-path compound duration (1h30m), single units (45s, 2m, 2h, 1d), and invalid inputs that throw.
Implementation is complete and tests cover happy-path combinations (1h30m, 2h15m30s), single units (1h, 30m, 45s), and invalid inputs that throw across multiple edge cases.
Implementation is present and working; tests use Jest framework and cover happy-path (1h30m), single units (2h, 45m, 30s), and invalid inputs with meaningful assertions.

No atomicity consideration for the redeem+credit pair: missing transaction, conditional UPDATE, or constraint note—concurrent requests can both pass the used-check and credit the balance, causing double-redemption.

Two separate queries with no transaction or update condition allow a single coupon to be credited twice in concurrent requests; lacks the required atomicity consideration (transaction, conditional update, or explicit constraint note).
Uses native <dialog> with showModal()/close(), zero dependencies, no custom modal infrastructure—only acceptable CSS styling.
Uses hand-rolled modal infrastructure (overlay div with z-index, manual display toggling) instead of native <dialog> or window.confirm() primitives.

Lacks atomicity consideration for the coupon validation and credit sequence—two concurrent requests can both SELECT the coupon as unused, then both UPDATE the user balance before either marks the coupon used, causing double-credit (classic TOCTOU race condition).
Uses custom overlay divs with z-index and manual display-state management instead of the native <dialog> element or window.confirm().
The custom limiter is a single-use factory parameterized for one endpoint, and lacks acknowledgment of its single-process ceiling or upgrade path in code or prose.

The solution includes a parameterized `rateLimit(maxRequests, windowMs)` factory for a single endpoint with fixed limits, which is an unrequested abstraction—write the middleware directly inline instead.

Hand-rolled limiter lacks any comment/note about its single-process ceiling or upgrade path (no 'swap to Redis when scaling' marker), and the parameterized factory createRateLimiter(maxRequests, windowMs) for one endpoint is an unrequested abstraction.
Uses Intl.RelativeTimeFormat for localization, includes no dependencies, and implements only a small unit-selection helper before calling the built-in API.
Uses Intl.RelativeTimeFormat with a small unit-selection helper, no external dependencies, no hand-maintained localization tables.
Uses Intl.RelativeTimeFormat with locale support, no external dependency, no manual translation tables, includes appropriate unit-selection helper logic.
Fix lands at the root `formatPrice` function with a null check, handling all call sites uniformly without symptom patching or duplication.
Fix lands at the root in the shared `formatPrice` function, handles all callers uniformly, explicitly avoids duplicating guards at every call site.
Fix lands at the root in the shared `formatPrice` function using `??` operator, handling null/undefined consistently for all callers without duplicating guards at call sites.
